input.content:
"""
Extract content from DOCX file for translation
"""
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Inches
import sys

def extract_docx_content(file_path):
    """Extract text and image information from DOCX file"""
    doc = Document(file_path)

    print("=" * 80)
    print("DOCUMENT STRUCTURE")
    print("=" * 80)

    for i, para in enumerate(doc.paragraphs):
        if para.text.strip():
            style = para.style.name if para.style else "Normal"
            print(f"\n[Paragraph {i+1}] [{style}]")
            print(f"Text: {para.text}")

    # Check for images
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("IMAGES")
    print("=" * 80)

    image_count = 0
    for rel in doc.part.rels.values():
        if "image" in rel.target_ref:
            image_count += 1
            print(f"Image {image_count}: {rel.target_ref}")

    if image_count == 0:
        print("No images found in document relationships.")

    # Check for tables
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("TABLES")
    print("=" * 80)

    if doc.tables:
        for i, table in enumerate(doc.tables):
            print(f"\n[Table {i+1}]")
            for row_idx, row in enumerate(table.rows):
                row_text = [cell.text for cell in row.cells]
                print(f"Row {row_idx + 1}: {' | '.join(row_text)}")
    else:
        print("No tables found.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python extract_docx_content.py <path_to_docx>")
        sys.exit(1)

    file_path = sys.argv[1]
    extract_docx_content(file_path)

"""
Translate IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number manual from Spanish to English
Preserves all images and formatting
"""
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Inches, Pt, RGBColor
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
import sys
import os

# Translation dictionary
translations = {
    # Document headers
    "Document summary": "Document summary",

def translate_text(text):
    """Translate Spanish text to English"""
    # Direct translation if found
    if text in translations:
        return translations[text]

    # Return original if not found (likely proper nouns or technical terms)
    return text

def create_english_version(source_path, dest_path):
    """Create English version of the document"""
    print(f"Reading source document: {source_path}")
    doc = Document(source_path)

    # Create new document with same styles
    new_doc = Document()

    # Copy all sections, preserving images and formatting
    print("Translating content...")

    for element in doc.element.body:
        # Handle paragraphs
        if element.tag.endswith('p'):
            # Find corresponding paragraph in doc
            para_index = [i for i, p in enumerate(doc.paragraphs) if p._element == element]
            if para_index:
                old_para = doc.paragraphs[para_index[0]]

                # Create new paragraph with same style
                new_para = new_doc.add_paragraph()
                new_para.style = old_para.style
                new_para.alignment = old_para.alignment

                # Translate text
                translated_text = translate_text(old_para.text)

                # Check if paragraph contains images
                if old_para._element.xpath('.//pic:pic'):
                    # Paragraph has images, need to preserve them
                    # Copy runs to preserve images
                    for run in old_para.runs:
                        new_run = new_para.add_run()

                        # Copy run properties
                        new_run.bold = run.bold
                        new_run.italic = run.italic
                        new_run.underline = run.underline
                        if run.font.size:
                            new_run.font.size = run.font.size
                        if run.font.name:
                            new_run.font.name = run.font.name

                        # Check if run contains image
                        if run._element.xpath('.//pic:pic'):
                            # Copy image
                            for drawing in run._element.xpath('.//w:drawing'):
                                new_run._element.append(drawing)
                        else:
                            # Translate text
                            new_run.text = translate_text(run.text)
                else:
                    # No images, just translate text
                    new_para.text = translated_text

                    # Copy formatting from first run if exists
                    if old_para.runs:
                        first_run = old_para.runs[0]
                        new_run = new_para.runs[0]
                        new_run.bold = first_run.bold
                        new_run.italic = first_run.italic
                        new_run.underline = first_run.underline
                        if first_run.font.size:
                            new_run.font.size = first_run.font.size
                        if first_run.font.name:
                            new_run.font.name = first_run.font.name

        # Handle tables
        elif element.tag.endswith('tbl'):
            # Find corresponding table in doc
            table_index = [i for i, t in enumerate(doc.tables) if t._element == element]
            if table_index:
                old_table = doc.tables[table_index[0]]

                # Create new table with same dimensions
                new_table = new_doc.add_table(rows=len(old_table.rows), cols=len(old_table.columns))
                new_table.style = old_table.style

                # Copy and translate cell content
                for row_idx, old_row in enumerate(old_table.rows):
                    for col_idx, old_cell in enumerate(old_row.cells):
                        new_cell = new_table.rows[row_idx].cells[col_idx]

                        # Translate cell text
                        translated = translate_text(old_cell.text)
                        new_cell.text = translated

                        # Copy cell formatting
                        if old_cell.paragraphs:
                            for para_idx, old_para in enumerate(old_cell.paragraphs):
                                if para_idx < len(new_cell.paragraphs):
                                    new_para = new_cell.paragraphs[para_idx]
                                    new_para.alignment = old_para.alignment

                                    # Copy run formatting
                                    if old_para.runs and new_para.runs:
                                        for run_idx, old_run in enumerate(old_para.runs):
                                            if run_idx < len(new_para.runs):
                                                new_run = new_para.runs[run_idx]
                                                new_run.bold = old_run.bold
                                                new_run.italic = old_run.italic
                                                if old_run.font.size:
                                                    new_run.font.size = old_run.font.size

    # Save the new document
    print(f"Saving English version to: {dest_path}")
    new_doc.save(dest_path)
    print("Done!")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    source = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals\IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number.docx"
    dest = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals\IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number (English).docx"

    create_english_version(source, dest)

def translate_text(text):
    """Translate Spanish text to English"""
    # Direct translation if found
    if text in translations:
        return translations[text]

    # Return original if not found (likely proper nouns or technical terms)
    return text

def create_english_version(source_path, dest_path):
    """Create English version of the document"""
    print(f"Reading source document: {source_path}")
    doc = Document(source_path)

    # Create new document with same styles
    new_doc = Document()

    # Copy all sections, preserving images and formatting
    print("Translating content...")

    for element in doc.element.body:
        # Handle paragraphs
        if element.tag.endswith('p'):
            # Find corresponding paragraph in doc
            para_index = [i for i, p in enumerate(doc.paragraphs) if p._element == element]
            if para_index:
                old_para = doc.paragraphs[para_index[0]]

                # Create new paragraph with same style
                new_para = new_doc.add_paragraph()
                new_para.style = old_para.style
                new_para.alignment = old_para.alignment

                # Translate text
                translated_text = translate_text(old_para.text)

                # Check if paragraph contains images
                if old_para._element.xpath('.//pic:pic'):
                    # Paragraph has images, need to preserve them
                    # Copy runs to preserve images
                    for run in old_para.runs:
                        new_run = new_para.add_run()

                        # Copy run properties
                        new_run.bold = run.bold
                        new_run.italic = run.italic
                        new_run.underline = run.underline
                        if run.font.size:
                            new_run.font.size = run.font.size
                        if run.font.name:
                            new_run.font.name = run.font.name

                        # Check if run contains image
                        if run._element.xpath('.//pic:pic'):
                            # Copy image
                            for drawing in run._element.xpath('.//w:drawing'):
                                new_run._element.append(drawing)
                        else:
                            # Translate text
                            new_run.text = translate_text(run.text)
                else:
                    # No images, just translate text
                    new_para.text = translated_text

                    # Copy formatting from first run if exists
                    if old_para.runs:
                        first_run = old_para.runs[0]
                        new_run = new_para.runs[0]
                        new_run.bold = first_run.bold
                        new_run.italic = first_run.italic
                        new_run.underline = first_run.underline
                        if first_run.font.size:
                            new_run.font.size = first_run.font.size
                        if first_run.font.name:
                            new_run.font.name = first_run.font.name

        # Handle tables
        elif element.tag.endswith('tbl'):
            # Find corresponding table in doc
            table_index = [i for i, t in enumerate(doc.tables) if t._element == element]
            if table_index:
                old_table = doc.tables[table_index[0]]

                # Create new table with same dimensions
                new_table = new_doc.add_table(rows=len(old_table.rows), cols=len(old_table.columns))
                new_table.style = old_table.style

                # Copy and translate cell content
                for row_idx, old_row in enumerate(old_table.rows):
                    for col_idx, old_cell in enumerate(old_row.cells):
                        new_cell = new_table.rows[row_idx].cells[col_idx]

                        # Translate cell text
                        translated = translate_text(old_cell.text)
                        new_cell.text = translated

                        # Copy cell formatting
                        if old_cell.paragraphs:
                            for para_idx, old_para in enumerate(old_cell.paragraphs):
                                if para_idx < len(new_cell.paragraphs):
                                    new_para = new_cell.paragraphs[para_idx]
                                    new_para.alignment = old_para.alignment

                                    # Copy run formatting
                                    if old_para.runs and new_para.runs:
                                        for run_idx, old_run in enumerate(old_para.runs):
                                            if run_idx < len(new_para.runs):
                                                new_run = new_para.runs[run_idx]
                                                new_run.bold = old_run.bold
                                                new_run.italic = old_run.italic
                                                if old_run.font.size:
                                                    new_run.font.size = old_run.font.size

    # Save the new document
    print(f"Saving English version to: {dest_path}")
    new_doc.save(dest_path)
    print("Done!")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    source = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals\IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number.docx"
    dest = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals\IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number (English).docx"

    create_english_version(source, dest)

"""
Extract DOCX content to Markdown with images
Translates Spanish to English for IT-030 W1
"""
from docx import Document
from docx.oxml import parse_xml
import os
import shutil

def extract_images_from_docx(docx_path, output_dir):
    """Extract all images from DOCX and save to output directory"""
    doc = Document(docx_path)

    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    image_map = {}  # Maps original filename to new filename
    image_counter = 1

    # Extract images from document relationships
    for rel in doc.part.rels.values():
        if "image" in rel.target_ref:
            # Get image binary data
            image_part = rel.target_part
            image_bytes = image_part.blob

            # Get file extension
            ext = rel.target_ref.split('.')[-1]

            # Create new filename
            new_filename = f"image{image_counter:02d}.{ext}"
            output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, new_filename)

            # Save image
            with open(output_path, 'wb') as img_file:
                img_file.write(image_bytes)

            # Map old to new filename
            image_map[rel.target_ref] = new_filename
            image_counter += 1

            print(f"Extracted: {new_filename}")

    return image_map

def get_paragraph_images(paragraph, image_map):
    """Get list of images in a paragraph"""
    images = []

    # Check for images in runs
    for run in paragraph.runs:
        # Look for drawing elements (images)
        drawings = run._element.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main}drawing')
        for drawing in drawings:
            # Find the image reference
            blips = drawing.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}blip')
            for blip in blips:
                embed = blip.get('{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships}embed')
                if embed:
                    # Find the relationship
                    for rel in paragraph._parent._parent.part.rels.values():
                        if rel.rId == embed:
                            if rel.target_ref in image_map:
                                images.append(image_map[rel.target_ref])

    return images

# English translations

}

# Content translations

def translate_text(text):
    """Translate text from Spanish to English"""
    text = text.strip()

    # Check direct translation
    if text in translations:
        return translations[text]

    if text in content_translations:
        return content_translations[text]

    # Return original if not found
    return text

def create_markdown_from_docx(docx_path, md_path, image_dir, image_map):
    """Create Markdown file from DOCX with English translations"""
    doc = Document(docx_path)

    md_content = []

    # Header
    md_content.append("# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("**Document ID:** IT-030 W1")
    md_content.append("**Application:** BeFirst")
    md_content.append("**Owner:** IT Department")
    md_content.append("**Status:** Active")
    md_content.append("**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("---")
    md_content.append("")

    # Document summary table
    md_content.append("## Document Summary")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("| Field | Value |")
    md_content.append("|-------|-------|")
    md_content.append("| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |")
    md_content.append("| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |")
    md_content.append("| **Version** | A |")
    md_content.append("| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |")
    md_content.append("| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("---")
    md_content.append("")

    # Process document content
    current_section = None
    image_counter = 1

    for para in doc.paragraphs:
        text = para.text.strip()

        if not text:
            continue

        # Check if it's a heading
        if para.style.name.startswith('Heading'):
            level = int(para.style.name.replace('Heading ', ''))
            translated = translate_text(text)
            md_content.append("")
            md_content.append(f"{'#' * (level + 1)} {translated}")
            md_content.append("")
            current_section = translated
        else:
            # Regular paragraph
            translated = translate_text(text)

            # Check for images in this paragraph
            images = get_paragraph_images(para, image_map)

            if images:
                # Add text first
                if translated:
                    md_content.append(translated)
                    md_content.append("")

                # Add images
                for img in images:
                    md_content.append(f"![Screenshot {image_counter}](assets/{img})")
                    md_content.append("")
                    image_counter += 1
            else:
                # Just text
                if para.style.name == 'List Paragraph':
                    md_content.append(f"- {translated}")
                else:
                    md_content.append(translated)
                    md_content.append("")

    # Write markdown file
    with open(md_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(md_content))

    print(f"Created Markdown file: {md_path}")

def main():
    # Paths
    docx_path = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals\IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number.docx"
    output_dir = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals_MD"
    assets_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "assets")
    md_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "IT-030_W1_BeFirst_Part_Number_Configuration.md")

    print("=" * 80)
    print("BeFirst IT-030 W1 - DOCX to Markdown Converter")
    print("=" * 80)
    print()

    # Extract images
    print("Step 1: Extracting images...")
    image_map = extract_images_from_docx(docx_path, assets_dir)
    print(f"Extracted {len(image_map)} images")
    print()

    # Create markdown
    print("Step 2: Creating Markdown file with English translations...")
    create_markdown_from_docx(docx_path, md_path, assets_dir, image_map)
    print()

    print("=" * 80)
    print("Conversion complete!")
    print(f"Markdown file: {md_path}")
    print(f"Images folder: {assets_dir}")
    print("=" * 80)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
Extract DOCX content to Markdown with images
Translates Spanish to English for IT-030 W1
"""
from docx import Document
from docx.oxml import parse_xml
import os
import shutil

def extract_images_from_docx(docx_path, output_dir):
    """Extract all images from DOCX and save to output directory"""
    doc = Document(docx_path)

    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    image_map = {}  # Maps original filename to new filename
    image_counter = 1

    # Extract images from document relationships
    for rel in doc.part.rels.values():
        if "image" in rel.target_ref:
            # Get image binary data
            image_part = rel.target_part
            image_bytes = image_part.blob

            # Get file extension
            ext = rel.target_ref.split('.')[-1]

            # Create new filename
            new_filename = f"image{image_counter:02d}.{ext}"
            output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, new_filename)

            # Save image
            with open(output_path, 'wb') as img_file:
                img_file.write(image_bytes)

            # Map old to new filename
            image_map[rel.target_ref] = new_filename
            image_counter += 1

            print(f"Extracted: {new_filename}")

    return image_map

def get_paragraph_images(paragraph, image_map):
    """Get list of images in a paragraph"""
    images = []

    # Check for images in runs
    for run in paragraph.runs:
        # Look for drawing elements (images)
        drawings = run._element.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main}drawing')
        for drawing in drawings:
            # Find the image reference
            blips = drawing.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}blip')
            for blip in blips:
                embed = blip.get('{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships}embed')
                if embed:
                    # Find the relationship
                    for rel in paragraph._parent._parent.part.rels.values():
                        if rel.rId == embed:
                            if rel.target_ref in image_map:
                                images.append(image_map[rel.target_ref])

    return images

    "Esta opción se utiliza para osram e imprime la lista de seriales de la caja en una hoja que se embarca a cliente.": "This option is used for Osram and prints the list of serials in the box on a sheet that is shipped to the customer.",
    "Solo pones el nombre de la impresora y unidades por hoja y presionas el botón (ADD)": "Simply enter the printer name and units per sheet, then press the **ADD** button.",
    "Si deseas eliminar la configuración presionas el botón (Delete)": "If you want to remove the configuration, press the **Delete** button.",
    "Esta opción es para que befirst escanee en automático en SMT": "This option allows BeFirst to scan automatically in SMT (Surface Mount Technology).",
    "Nota para esta opción tenemos que asegurar que la AOI lea los QR y sean enviados a BEFIRST": "**Note:** For this option, ensure that the AOI reads QR codes and sends them to BeFirst.",
    "Para configurarla solo seleccionamos área y lado y presionamos en el botón ADD": "To configure it, select area and side, then press the **ADD** button.",
    "Para eliminarla solo presionamos el botón ADD": "To remove it, press the **ADD** button.",
    "Esta opción es para pedir que befirst requiera que las piezas tengan registro de AOI": "This option requires BeFirst to verify that pieces have an AOI record.",
    "Seleccionamos el área y lado donde queremos que solicite el escaneo de AOI después presionamos el botón ADD": "Select the area and side where you want to require AOI scanning, then press the **ADD** button.",
    "Si deseamos eliminar esta opción presionamos el botón DElete": "If you want to remove this option, press the **Delete** button.",
}

def translate_text(text):
    """Translate text from Spanish to English"""
    text = text.strip()

    # Check direct translation
    if text in translations:
        return translations[text]

    if text in content_translations:
        return content_translations[text]

    # Return original if not found
    return text

def create_markdown_from_docx(docx_path, md_path, image_dir, image_map):
    """Create Markdown file from DOCX with English translations"""
    doc = Document(docx_path)

    md_content = []

    # Header
    md_content.append("# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("**Document ID:** IT-030 W1")
    md_content.append("**Application:** BeFirst")
    md_content.append("**Owner:** IT Department")
    md_content.append("**Status:** Active")
    md_content.append("**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("---")
    md_content.append("")

    # Document summary table
    md_content.append("## Document Summary")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("| Field | Value |")
    md_content.append("|-------|-------|")
    md_content.append("| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |")
    md_content.append("| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |")
    md_content.append("| **Version** | A |")
    md_content.append("| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |")
    md_content.append("| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |")
    md_content.append("")
    md_content.append("---")
    md_content.append("")

    # Process document content
    current_section = None
    image_counter = 1

    for para in doc.paragraphs:
        text = para.text.strip()

        if not text:
            continue

        # Check if it's a heading
        if para.style.name.startswith('Heading'):
            level = int(para.style.name.replace('Heading ', ''))
            translated = translate_text(text)
            md_content.append("")
            md_content.append(f"{'#' * (level + 1)} {translated}")
            md_content.append("")
            current_section = translated
        else:
            # Regular paragraph
            translated = translate_text(text)

            # Check for images in this paragraph
            images = get_paragraph_images(para, image_map)

            if images:
                # Add text first
                if translated:
                    md_content.append(translated)
                    md_content.append("")

                # Add images
                for img in images:
                    md_content.append(f"![Screenshot {image_counter}](assets/{img})")
                    md_content.append("")
                    image_counter += 1
            else:
                # Just text
                if para.style.name == 'List Paragraph':
                    md_content.append(f"- {translated}")
                else:
                    md_content.append(translated)
                    md_content.append("")

    # Write markdown file
    with open(md_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(md_content))

    print(f"Created Markdown file: {md_path}")

def main():
    # Paths
    docx_path = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals\IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number.docx"
    output_dir = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals_MD"
    assets_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "assets")
    md_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "IT-030_W1_BeFirst_Part_Number_Configuration.md")

    print("=" * 80)
    print("BeFirst IT-030 W1 - DOCX to Markdown Converter")
    print("=" * 80)
    print()

    # Extract images
    print("Step 1: Extracting images...")
    image_map = extract_images_from_docx(docx_path, assets_dir)
    print(f"Extracted {len(image_map)} images")
    print()

    # Create markdown
    print("Step 2: Creating Markdown file with English translations...")
    create_markdown_from_docx(docx_path, md_path, assets_dir, image_map)
    print()

    print("=" * 80)
    print("Conversion complete!")
    print(f"Markdown file: {md_path}")
    print(f"Images folder: {assets_dir}")
    print("=" * 80)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

file.content:
# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration

**Document ID:** IT-030 W1
**Application:** BeFirst
**Owner:** IT Department
**Status:** Active
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11

---

## Document Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |
| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |
| **Version** | A |
| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |
| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |

---

Document summary

Document history


## General

- In this option you can modify the following fields:
- PartType
- Component=número de parte de un componente
- **Wip** = Shop floor part number
- CustomerID
- Change customer
- ISactive
- Deactivate part number
- Facility
- Choose between Juarez or El Paso
- PartDesc
- Add a description to the part number

## Image

- This option allows you to add an image to the product. Simply select the image and click the save button.

## Serialization

- In this option you can modify the following fields:
- Laser
- Indicate when the model is Laser
- LabelByPanel
- Enable label printing by panel
- LabelsID
- Enter label name
- PIecesBy Panel
- Number of pieces per panel
- Merge serials
- Perform merge
- Generate Panel Master Automatically
- Generate panel when serializing
- PartSerialFormatEasy
- Individual format
- PartSerialPanelFormatEasy
- Panel format

## Tools Validation

- In this option you can add solder paste, conformal coating, and parameter sheets.

## Packout Options

- In this option you can modify the following fields:
- Price
- Add the piece price
- Cost
- Add the piece cost
- HavePVS
- Add when you want it scanned in PVS
- BoxSize
- Box size (pieces per box)
- Layers
- Number of layers per box
- Layers_Allow_Duplicate
- Allow duplicate layers
- PackRowSize
- Fense required before packout
- Allowed test time
- Default Stock Location
- PackType
- Type of scan to perform
- Quantity Per Scan
- Quantity of pieces per scan
- **Option: Serial modify - Substring section**
- In this option, the serial number at all stations is `C310820101381`, but at packing it converts to `#80A906093C    ##812774456#060920*X57 A3B3003FVG3*=300820102045C80A`. With this option, we can make BeFirst read part of the serial. For this case:
- In **Area**: Select `PackOUT`
- In **Format**: Enter `(51,2)` which means (Start position, Total characters)
- **Option: Defines the default number of partials allowed**

This option defines how many partial boxes can be open simultaneously.

# IT Documentation Control System

**Created:** 2026-05-11
**Owner:** IT Department

---

## Quick Reference

### Document ID Format

```
IT-XXX          → General IT documents (policies, procedures)
IT-XXX W1       → Work instructions/manuals (BeFirst, Plex)
IT-XXX W1 FN    → Multiple topics under same procedure (F1, F2, F3...)
```

### Filename Convention

```
ID_AppName_Description.md
```

**Examples:**
- `IT-030_W1_BeFirst_Part_Number_Configuration.md`
- `IT-030_W1_F1_BeFirst_General_Settings.md`
- `IT-005_IT_Network_Security_Policy.md`

---

## Directory Structure

```
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\
│
├── Enterprise Solution Architecture\
│   └── Enterprise Systems\
│       ├── BeFirst\
│       │   ├── 01_User_Manuals\              ← Legacy DOCX files
│       │   └── 01_User_Manuals_MD\           ← New MD files ✓
│       │       └── assets\                   ← Screenshots/images
│       │
│       └── Plex\
│           ├── 01_User_Manuals\              ← Legacy DOCX files
│           └── 01_User_Manuals_MD\           ← New MD files ✓
│               └── assets\                   ← Screenshots/images
│
└── Grand Rapids IT Hub\
    ├── 01_Policies\                          ← IT policies (IT-XXX)
    ├── 02_Procedures\                        ← IT procedures (IT-XXX)
    ├── DOCUMENT-ID-CONTROL.md                ← Main registry ✓
    └── README-DOCUMENT-CONTROL.md            ← This file ✓
```

---

## How to Use

### Creating New Documents

1. Check **DOCUMENT-ID-CONTROL.md** for next available ID
2. Create MD file with naming convention: `ID_AppName_Description.md`
3. Save screenshots to `assets/` subfolder
4. Update **DOCUMENT-ID-CONTROL.md** with new entry
5. Set **Status** to "New (MD)" or "Draft"

### Converting Existing DOCX Documents

1. Assign new ID from control file
2. Use conversion script or manual conversion
3. Save as `ID_AppName_Description.md`
4. Extract images to `assets/` folder
5. Update control file with **Status: "Migrated"**

### Document Status Values

- **New (MD)** - New Markdown document
- **Migrated** - Converted from DOCX to MD
- **Legacy (DOCX)** - Still in old location
- **Draft** - Work in progress
- **Archived** - Superseded by newer version

---

## Example: IT-030 W1

**First BeFirst manual created:**

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | IT-030 W1 |
| **Title** | Part Number Configuration |
| **App** | BeFirst |
| **Owner** | IT |
| **Status** | New (MD) |
| **Filename** | `IT-030_W1_BeFirst_Part_Number_Configuration.md` |
| **Path** | BeFirst\01_User_Manuals_MD |
| **Images** | 36 screenshots in assets/ folder |

---

## Next Available IDs

- **BeFirst:** IT-031 W1
- **Plex:** IT-030 W1
- **IT General:** IT-030

---

## Tools

- **Markdown Editor:** Typora, VS Code, Obsidian
- **Conversion Script:** `BeFirst_Docs\scripts\extract_docx_to_md.py`
- **Version Control:** OneDrive (automatic)

---

## Contact

**IT Department**
Email: GR_IT@safaricircuits.com

# IT Documentation Control System

**Created:** 2026-05-11
**Owner:** IT Department

---

## Quick Reference

### Document ID Format

```
IT-XXX          → General IT documents (policies, procedures)
IT-XXX W1       → Work instructions/manuals (BeFirst, Plex)
IT-XXX W1 FN    → Multiple topics under same procedure (F1, F2, F3...)
```

### Filename Convention

```
ID_AppName_Description.md
```

**Examples:**
- `IT-030_W1_BeFirst_Part_Number_Configuration.md`
- `IT-030_W1_F1_BeFirst_General_Settings.md`
- `IT-005_IT_Network_Security_Policy.md`

---

## Directory Structure

```
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\
│
├── Enterprise Solution Architecture\
│   └── Enterprise Systems\
│       ├── BeFirst\
│       │   ├── 01_User_Manuals\              ← Legacy DOCX files
│       │   └── 01_User_Manuals_MD\           ← New MD files ✓
│       │       └── assets\                   ← Screenshots/images
│       │
│       └── Plex\
│           ├── 01_User_Manuals\              ← Legacy DOCX files
│           └── 01_User_Manuals_MD\           ← New MD files ✓
│               └── assets\                   ← Screenshots/images
│
└── Grand Rapids IT Hub\
    ├── 01_Policies\                          ← IT policies (IT-XXX)
    ├── 02_Procedures\                        ← IT procedures (IT-XXX)
    ├── DOCUMENT-ID-CONTROL.md                ← Main registry ✓
    └── README-DOCUMENT-CONTROL.md            ← This file ✓
```

---

## How to Use

### Creating New Documents

1. Check **DOCUMENT-ID-CONTROL.md** for next available ID
2. Create MD file with naming convention: `ID_AppName_Description.md`
3. Save screenshots to `assets/` subfolder
4. Update **DOCUMENT-ID-CONTROL.md** with new entry
5. Set **Status** to "New (MD)" or "Draft"

### Converting Existing DOCX Documents

1. Assign new ID from control file
2. Use conversion script or manual conversion
3. Save as `ID_AppName_Description.md`
4. Extract images to `assets/` folder
5. Update control file with **Status: "Migrated"**

### Document Status Values

- **New (MD)** - New Markdown document
- **Migrated** - Converted from DOCX to MD
- **Legacy (DOCX)** - Still in old location
- **Draft** - Work in progress
- **Archived** - Superseded by newer version

---

| **Path** | BeFirst\01_User_Manuals_MD |
| **Images** | 36 screenshots in assets/ folder |

---

## Next Available IDs

- **BeFirst:** IT-031 W1
- **Plex:** IT-030 W1
- **IT General:** IT-030

---

## Tools

- **Markdown Editor:** Typora, VS Code, Obsidian
- **Conversion Script:** `BeFirst_Docs\scripts\extract_docx_to_md.py`
- **Version Control:** OneDrive (automatic)

---

## Contact

**IT Department**

1→# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration
     2→
     3→**Document ID:** IT-030 W1
     4→**Application:** BeFirst
     5→**Owner:** IT Department
     6→**Status:** Active
     7→**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Document Summary
    12→
    13→| Field | Value |
    14→|-------|-------|
    15→| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |
    16→| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |
    17→| **Version** | A |
    18→| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |
    19→| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→Document summary
    24→
    25→Document history
    26→
    27→
    28→## General
    29→
    30→- In this option you can modify the following fields:
    31→- PartType
    32→- Component=número de parte de un componente
    33→- **Wip** = Shop floor part number
    34→- CustomerID
    35→- Change customer
    36→- ISactive
    37→- Deactivate part number
    38→- Facility
    39→- Choose between Juarez or El Paso
    40→- PartDesc
    41→- Add a description to the part number
    42→
    43→## Image
    44→
    45→- This option allows you to add an image to the product. Simply select the image and click the save button.
    46→
    47→## Serialization
    48→
    49→- In this option you can modify the following fields:
    50→- Laser
    51→- Indicate when the model is Laser
    52→- LabelByPanel
    53→- Enable label printing by panel
    54→- LabelsID
    55→- Enter label name
    56→- PIecesBy Panel
    57→- Number of pieces per panel
    58→- Merge serials
    59→- Perform merge
    60→- Generate Panel Master Automatically
    61→- Generate panel when serializing
    62→- PartSerialFormatEasy
    63→- Individual format
    64→- PartSerialPanelFormatEasy
    65→- Panel format
    66→
    67→## Tools Validation
    68→
    69→- In this option you can add solder paste, conformal coating, and parameter sheets.
    70→
    71→## Packout Options
    72→
    73→- In this option you can modify the following fields:
    74→- Price
    75→- Add the piece price
    76→- Cost
    77→- Add the piece cost
    78→- HavePVS
    79→- Add when you want it scanned in PVS
    80→- BoxSize
    81→- Box size (pieces per box)
    82→- Layers
    83→- Number of layers per box
    84→- Layers_Allow_Duplicate
    85→- Allow duplicate layers
    86→- PackRowSize
    87→- Fense required before packout
    88→- Allowed test time
    89→- Default Stock Location
    90→- PackType
    91→- Type of scan to perform
    92→- Quantity Per Scan
    93→- Quantity of pieces per scan
    94→- **Option: Serial modify - Substring section**
    95→- In this option, the serial number at all stations is `C310820101381`, but at packing it converts to `#80A906093C    ##812774456#060920*X57 A3B3003FVG3*=300820102045C80A`. With this option, we can make BeFirst read part of the serial. For this case:
    96→- In **Area**: Select `PackOUT`
    97→- In **Format**: Enter `(51,2)` which means (Start position, Total characters)
    98→- **Option: Defines the default number of partials allowed**
    99→
   100→This option defines how many partial boxes can be open simultaneously.
   101→- Simply enter the number of boxes and select **Add**.
   102→- If you want to remove this option, press the **Delete** button.
   103→- **Option: Serial modify - Supstring section**
   104→- Following the previous serial example `#80A906093C    ##812774456#060920*X57 A3B3003FVG3*=300820102045C80A`, we will take the format `(51,2)` added in SUBSTRING but add `C` prefix and modify it as follows:
   105→- - In **Area**: Select `Packout`
   106→- In **SupString**: Enter `C{SN}`
   107→- This option is used for AUDI parts.
   108→- **Scanning a component at packing**
   109→- Example for Osram: Scan a cable with code `12345679`. With this option:
   110→- In **Format** field: Add the code format
   111→- In **Title**: Enter the component name you want to scan at packing
   112→- **Option: Validate screw** - This option is for GR (Grand Rapids).
   113→- **Option: Layers**
   114→- This option is activated and configured as follows:
   115→- OOBA
   116→- Define if scanning in OOBA
   117→- LayersInicio
   118→- Indicates the number of empty trays or lids at the beginning
   119→- LayersIniciotype
   120→- Define whether tray or lid will be scanned
   121→- LayersFinal
   122→- Indicates the number of empty trays or lids at the end
   123→- LayerCode
   124→- Format of tray or lid code
   125→
   126→## Areas by Panel
   127→
   128→- In this option you will add the areas where scanning will be done by panel.
   129→- Simply select the area and press the **ADD** button.
   130→- If you want to remove a panel area, press the **Delete** button.

file.content:
# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration

**Document ID:** IT-030 W1
**Application:** BeFirst
**Owner:** IT Department
**Status:** Active
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11

---

## Document Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |
| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |
| **Version** | A |
| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |
| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |

---

Document summary

Document history


## General

- In this option you can modify the following fields:
- PartType
- Component=número de parte de un componente
- **Wip** = Shop floor part number
- CustomerID
- Change customer
- ISactive
- Deactivate part number
- Facility
- Choose between Juarez or El Paso
- PartDesc
- Add a description to the part number

## Image

- This option allows you to add an image to the product. Simply select the image and click the save button.

## Serialization

- In this option you can modify the following fields:
- Laser
- Indicate when the model is Laser
- LabelByPanel
- Enable label printing by panel
- LabelsID
- Enter label name
- PIecesBy Panel
- Number of pieces per panel
- Merge serials
- Perform merge
- Generate Panel Master Automatically
- Generate panel when serializing
- PartSerialFormatEasy
- Individual format
- PartSerialPanelFormatEasy
- Panel format

## Tools Validation

- In this option you can add solder paste, conformal coating, and parameter sheets.

## Packout Options

- In this option you can modify the following fields:
- Price
- Add the piece price
- Cost
- Add the piece cost
- HavePVS
- Add when you want it scanned in PVS
- BoxSize
- Box size (pieces per box)
- Layers
- Number of layers per box
- Layers_Allow_Duplicate
- Allow duplicate layers
- PackRowSize
- Fense required before packout
- Allowed test time
- Default Stock Location
- PackType
- Type of scan to perform
- Quantity Per Scan
- Quantity of pieces per scan
- **Option: Serial modify - Substring section**
- In this option, the serial number at all stations is `C310820101381`, but at packing it converts to `#80A906093C    ##812774456#060920*X57 A3B3003FVG3*=300820102045C80A`. With this option, we can make BeFirst read part of the serial. For this case:
- In **Area**: Select `PackOUT`
- In **Format**: Enter `(51,2)` which means (Start position, Total characters)
- **Option: Defines the default number of partials allowed**

This option defines how many partial boxes can be open simultaneously.
- Simply enter the number of boxes and select **Add**.
- If you want to remove this option, press the **Delete** button.
- **Option: Serial modify - Supstring section**
- Following the previous serial example `#80A906093C    ##812774456#060920*X57 A3B3003FVG3*=300820102045C80A`, we will take the format `(51,2)` added in SUBSTRING but add `C` prefix and modify it as follows:
- - In **Area**: Select `Packout`
- In **SupString**: Enter `C{SN}`
- This option is used for AUDI parts.
- **Scanning a component at packing**
- Example for Osram: Scan a cable with code `12345679`. With this option:
- In **Format** field: Add the code format
- In **Title**: Enter the component name you want to scan at packing
- **Option: Validate screw** - This option is for GR (Grand Rapids).
- **Option: Layers**
- This option is activated and configured as follows:
- OOBA
- Define if scanning in OOBA
- LayersInicio
- Indicates the number of empty trays or lids at the beginning
- LayersIniciotype
- Define whether tray or lid will be scanned
- LayersFinal
- Indicates the number of empty trays or lids at the end
- LayerCode
- Format of tray or lid code

## Areas by Panel

- In this option you will add the areas where scanning will be done by panel.
- Simply select the area and press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove a panel area, press the **Delete** button.

## Areas with Gages

- In this option you will add Gages and the area where they will be used.
- First select the area where it will be used
- The gage name
- If you want to remove the gage, press the **delete** button.

## Areas Formats

- This option is used when the serial number format changes between stations.
- Select the area where it applies
- Add the new serial number format and press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove the format, press the **Delete** button.

## Areas with Pallets

- This option is used to define a pallet format in a specific area.
- Select the area where it applies
- Add the pallet format and press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove the format, press the **Delete** button.

## Areas Locks

- This option is exclusive to BeFirst administrators.

## 2 Min Defects

- This option applies when you want an area to be locked if there is a failure within 15 minutes difference.
- Select the area where it applies and press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove the area, press the **Delete** button.

## 2 Custom Defects

- In this option you can add specific defects to the part number.
- Simply select the defect and press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove it, press the **delete** button.

## 2 Row Label Test

- This option is exclusive to BeFirst administrators.

## 2 Reprint Options Validations

- This option is for validating label reprints.
- Select the area where it applies
- Add the format and press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove the format, press the **Delete** button.

## 2 Label Shipping Validation

- This option assigns the validation order of labels and the format they should have.
- Add the label type
- Add a format
- And the order in which it will be validated
- If you want to delete any element, press the **delete** button.

## BOM

- This option displays the BOM (Bill of Materials) for the part number.
- If any element is not found, click the **Update** button.

## Print Option

- Define the printers to be used in MLS (Blue and Green labels).
- Select the station where printing will occur
- Add the printer name
- Leave **Force Values** blank
- Press **ADD**
- If you want to remove the printer, press the **delete** button.

## Packout Alternate

- This option checks the part number components and verifies if it has alternates. If it does, it will print a label indicating alternates.
- Here you only need to press the **ADD** button.
- To cancel the option, press the **delete** button.

## Approved Stations

- With this option, the part can only run in the selected area.
- Select area
- Select station
- Press the **ADD** button
- If you want to deactivate this option, press the **Delete** button.

## Parameter Stations

- This option is for adding parameter sheets.
- Seleccionamos área
- Select station
- Select side
- Select the parameter sheet
- Then press the **ADD** button
- If you want to remove any parameter, press the **delete** button.

## Routing

- This option is used to view BeFirst routes and update them, define packout, or skip previous station.
- If you only want to update routes, press the **update** button.
- If you want to skip the previous station, select area, side, and the date until it will be active.
- If you want to cancel this option, press the **Clear Skips** button.
- If you want to define the route as packout, press **Set Packout**.

## Rates

- This option is for loading production rates.
- Select area, station, side and add the rate. Once all fields are filled, press the **ADD** button.

## Assembly Instructions

- This option is for loading visual aids (work instructions).
- Ahora llenamos los siguientes datos (área, Subastación, Lado comentarios y cargamos la instrucción de trabajo )
- Once all fields are filled, press the **ADD** button.
- The work instruction does not require approval; it will be automatically approved upon creation.

## Groups

- This option is for adding groups to part numbers.
- In **GroupName**, add the group name.
- If you want to remove a group, press the **delete** button.

## Bins

- This option is for adding BINS to part numbers.
- In **BINNAME**, add the BIN name.
- If conversion applies, fill that field.
- If you want to remove a BIN, press the **delete** button.

## Group Bins

- This option is for adding Group BINS to part numbers.
- Select the group, part number, Bin, and if it is parent. Also choose the number of times it will be scanned.

## Printers

- This option is used for Osram and prints the list of serials in the box on a sheet that is shipped to the customer.
- Simply enter the printer name and units per sheet, then press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove the configuration, press the **Delete** button.

## Force AOI

- This option allows BeFirst to scan automatically in SMT (Surface Mount Technology).
- **Note:** For this option, ensure that the AOI reads QR codes and sends them to BeFirst.
- To configure it, select area and side, then press the **ADD** button.
- To remove it, press the **ADD** button.

## Search AOI

- This option requires BeFirst to verify that pieces have an AOI record.
- Select the area and side where you want to require AOI scanning, then press the **ADD** button.
- If you want to remove this option, press the **Delete** button.

"""
Rebuild IT-030 W1 Markdown with proper image placement and complete translations
"""
from docx import Document
import os

def extract_images(docx_path, output_dir):
    """Extract all images from DOCX"""
    doc = Document(docx_path)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    image_counter = 1
    for rel in doc.part.rels.values():
        if "image" in rel.target_ref:
            image_part = rel.target_part
            image_bytes = image_part.blob
            ext = rel.target_ref.split('.')[-1]
            filename = f"image{image_counter:02d}.{ext}"

            with open(os.path.join(output_dir, filename), 'wb') as f:
                f.write(image_bytes)

            print(f"Extracted: {filename}")
            image_counter += 1

    return image_counter - 1

def create_comprehensive_markdown():
    """Create complete markdown with all sections and images"""

    md = """# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration

**Document ID:** IT-030 W1
**Application:** BeFirst
**Owner:** IT Department
**Status:** Active
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11

---

## Document Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |
| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |
| **Version** | A |
| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |
| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |

---

## Table of Contents

1. [General](#general)
2. [Image](#image)
3. [Serialization](#serialization)
4. [Tools Validation](#tools-validation)
5. [Packout Options](#packout-options)
6. [Areas by Panel](#areas-by-panel)
7. [Areas with Gages](#areas-with-gages)
8. [Areas Formats](#areas-formats)
9. [Areas with Pallets](#areas-with-pallets)
10. [Areas Locks](#areas-locks)
11. [2 Min Defects](#2-min-defects)
12. [2 Custom Defects](#2-custom-defects)
13. [2 Row Label Test](#2-row-label-test)
14. [2 Reprint Options Validations](#2-reprint-options-validations)
15. [2 Label Shipping Validation](#2-label-shipping-validation)
16. [BOM](#bom)
17. [Print Option](#print-option)
18. [Packout Alternate](#packout-alternate)
19. [Approved Stations](#approved-stations)
20. [Parameter Stations](#parameter-stations)
21. [Routing](#routing)
22. [Rates](#rates)
23. [Assembly Instructions](#assembly-instructions)
24. [Groups](#groups)
25. [Bins](#bins)
26. [Group Bins](#group-bins)
27. [Printers](#printers)
28. [Force AOI](#force-aoi)
29. [Search AOI](#search-aoi)

---

## General

In this option you can modify the following fields:

### PartType
- **Component** = Component part number
- **Wip** = Shop floor part number

![General Settings](assets/image01.png)

### CustomerID
Change the customer assigned to the part number.

### ISactive
Deactivate or activate the part number.

### Facility
Choose between Juarez (JUA1/JUA2) or El Paso.

![Facility Selection](assets/image02.png)

### PartDesc
Add a description to the part number.

---

## Image

This option allows you to add an image to the product. Simply select the image and click the **Save** button.

![Image Upload Section](assets/image03.png)

**Steps:**
1. Click **Choose File** or drag and drop image
2. Preview the image
3. Click **Save** to apply

---

## Serialization

In this option you can modify the following serialization fields:

![Serialization Settings](assets/image04.png)

### Laser
Indicate when the model uses Laser marking.

### LabelByPanel
Enable label printing by panel instead of individual pieces.

### LabelsID
Enter the label template name to be used.

![Label Configuration](assets/image05.png)

### PiecesBy Panel
Number of pieces per panel.

### Merge Serials
Enable merging of serial numbers.

### Generate Panel Master Automatically
Generate the panel master serial when serializing individual pieces.

![Panel Settings](assets/image06.png)

### PartSerialFormatEasy
Individual serial number format (regex pattern).

### PartSerialPanelFormatEasy
Panel serial number format (regex pattern).

---

## Tools Validation

In this option you can add:
- Solder paste validations
- Conformal coating requirements
- Parameter sheets

![Tools Validation](assets/image07.png)

Select the required tools and validation parameters for the part number.

---

## Packout Options

Configure packing and shipping settings:

![Packout Options Overview](assets/image08.png)

### Price
Add the piece price for cost tracking.

### Cost
Add the piece cost.

### HavePVS
Enable scanning in PVS (Packout Verification Software).

![PVS Settings](assets/image09.png)

### BoxSize
Number of pieces per box.

### Layers
Number of layers per box.

### Layers_Allow_Duplicate
Allow duplicate serial numbers in different layers.

![Box Configuration](assets/image10.png)

### PackRowSize
Number of pieces per row in packing.

### Fense Required Before Packout
Minimum time required after testing before packing (in hours).

### Default Stock Location
Default inventory location after packing.

### PackType
Type of scan to perform:
- **Serial** - Scan individual serials
- **Quantity** - Scan by quantity
- **Panel** - Scan panel serials

![Pack Type Selection](assets/image11.png)

### Quantity Per Scan
Number of pieces to add per scan when using Quantity pack type.

---

### Serial Modify Options

#### Substring Option

![Substring Configuration](assets/image12.png)

In this option, the serial number at all stations might be different from the serial at packing.

**Example:**
- Station serial: `C310820101381`
- Packing serial: `#80A906093C    ##812774456#060920*X57 A3B3003FVG3*=300820102045C80A`

With this option, BeFirst can read part of the serial:

**Configuration:**
- **Area**: Select `PackOUT`
- **Format**: Enter `(51,2)` which means:
  - Position 51 = Start position
  - 2 = Total characters to read

![Substring Example](assets/image13.png)

#### Supstring Option

![Supstring Configuration](assets/image14.png)

Following the previous example, if you need to add a prefix to the extracted serial:

**Configuration:**
- **Area**: Select `Packout`
- **SupString**: Enter `C{SN}` (adds "C" before the serial number)

This option is commonly used for AUDI parts.

---

### Partial Boxes Configuration

![Partial Boxes](assets/image15.png)

**Defines the default number of partials allowed**

This option defines how many partial boxes can be open simultaneously.

**Steps:**
1. Enter the number of allowed partial boxes
2. Click **Add**
3. To remove, click **Delete**

---

### Component Scanning at Packing

![Component Scanning](assets/image16.png)

Scan specific components during packing process.

**Example for Osram:**
Scan a cable with code `12345679` at packing.

**Configuration:**
- **Format**: Add the component barcode format
- **Title**: Enter the component name

![Component Example](assets/image17.png)

---

### Validate Screw Option

![Validate Screw](assets/image18.png)

This option is specific to Grand Rapids (GR) facility.
Validates that screws are properly installed before packing.

---

### Layers Configuration

![Layers Configuration](assets/image19.png)

This option configures empty trays/lids in boxes.

**Fields:**

#### OOBA
Define if the part scans in OOBA (Out of Box Audit).

#### LayersInicio
Number of empty trays or lids at the beginning of the box.

#### LayersIniciotype
Define whether tray or lid will be scanned at beginning.

#### LayersFinal
Number of empty trays or lids at the end of the box.

![Layers Detail](assets/image20.png)

#### LayerCode
Format of the tray or lid barcode.

**Example:** `^TRAY\d{6}$` for 6-digit tray codes

---

## Areas by Panel

Configure which manufacturing areas scan by panel instead of individual pieces.

![Areas by Panel](assets/image21.png)

**Steps:**
1. Select the area from dropdown
2. Click **ADD**
3. To remove an area, select it and click **Delete**

---

## Areas with Gages

Configure gages (measurement tools) required for specific areas.

![Areas with Gages](assets/image22.png)

**Steps:**
1. Select the area where gage will be used
2. Enter the gage name or ID
3. Click **ADD**
4. To remove, select the gage and click **Delete**

---

## Areas Formats

Use when the serial number format changes between manufacturing stations.

![Areas Formats](assets/image23.png)

**Steps:**
1. Select the area where format change applies
2. Enter the new serial number format (regex)
3. Click **ADD**
4. To remove, select format and click **Delete**

---

## Areas with Pallets

Define pallet format requirements for specific areas.

![Areas with Pallets](assets/image24.png)

**Steps:**
1. Select the area
2. Enter the pallet barcode format
3. Click **ADD**
4. To remove, select and click **Delete**

---

## Areas Locks

![Areas Locks](assets/image25.png)

**Administrator Only Feature**

This option is exclusive to BeFirst administrators for locking/unlocking manufacturing areas.

---

## 2 Min Defects

Lock an area if the same defect occurs within 15 minutes.

![2 Min Defects](assets/image26.png)

**Steps:**
1. Select the area to monitor
2. Click **ADD**
3. To remove, select area and click **Delete**

This helps identify systemic issues quickly.

---

## 2 Custom Defects

Add specific defect codes for this part number.

![2 Custom Defects](assets/image27.png)

**Steps:**
1. Select the defect from the list
2. Click **ADD**
3. To remove, select defect and click **Delete**

---

## 2 Row Label Test

![2 Row Label Test](assets/image28.png)

**Administrator Only Feature**

Used for testing label printing functionality.

---

## 2 Reprint Options Validations

Configure validation requirements for label reprints.

![2 Reprint Options](assets/image29.png)

**Steps:**
1. Select the area
2. Enter the validation format
3. Click **ADD**
4. To remove, select and click **Delete**

---

## 2 Label Shipping Validation

Configure shipping label validation order and formats.

![Label Shipping Validation](assets/image30.png)

**Steps:**
1. Add label type
2. Enter format pattern
3. Specify validation order (1, 2, 3...)

## Parameter Stations

## Rates

Load production rates for stations.

**Steps:**

2. Select station
3. Select side

## Assembly Instructions

Upload visual aids (work instructions) for stations.

3. Select side

## Groups

Create groups to organize part numbers.

## Bins

Add inventory bins for part numbers.

**Steps:**

## Group Bins

Assign bins to groups with parent-child relationships.

"""
Rebuild IT-030 W1 Markdown with proper image placement and complete translations
"""
from docx import Document
import os

def extract_images(docx_path, output_dir):
    """Extract all images from DOCX"""
    doc = Document(docx_path)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    image_counter = 1
    for rel in doc.part.rels.values():
        if "image" in rel.target_ref:
            image_part = rel.target_part
            image_bytes = image_part.blob
            ext = rel.target_ref.split('.')[-1]
            filename = f"image{image_counter:02d}.{ext}"

            with open(os.path.join(output_dir, filename), 'wb') as f:
                f.write(image_bytes)

            print(f"Extracted: {filename}")
            image_counter += 1

    return image_counter - 1

def create_comprehensive_markdown():
    """Create complete markdown with all sections and images"""

    md = """# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration

**Document ID:** IT-030 W1
**Application:** BeFirst
**Owner:** IT Department
**Status:** Active
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11

| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |

2. [Image](#image)

10. [Areas Locks](#areas-locks)
11. [2 Min Defects](#2-min-defects)
12. [2 Custom Defects](#2-custom-defects)
13. [2 Row Label Test](#2-row-label-test)
14. [2 Reprint Options Validations](#2-reprint-options-validations)
15. [2 Label Shipping Validation](#2-label-shipping-validation)
16. [BOM](#bom)
17. [Print Option](#print-option)

19. [Approved Stations](#approved-stations)
20. [Parameter Stations](#parameter-stations)
21. [Routing](#routing)
22. [Rates](#rates)
23. [Assembly Instructions](#assembly-instructions)
24. [Groups](#groups)
25. [Bins](#bins)
26. [Group Bins](#group-bins)
27. [Printers](#printers)
28. [Force AOI](#force-aoi)
29. [Search AOI](#search-aoi)

---

## General

In this option you can modify the following fields:

### PartType
- **Component** = Component part number
- **Wip** = Shop floor part number

![General Settings](assets/image01.png)

### CustomerID
Change the customer assigned to the part number.

### ISactive
Deactivate or activate the part number.

### Facility
Choose between Juarez (JUA1/JUA2) or El Paso.

![Facility Selection](assets/image02.png)

### PartDesc
Add a description to the part number.

---

## Image

This option allows you to add an image to the product. Simply select the image and click the **Save** button.

![Image Upload Section](assets/image03.png)

**Steps:**
1. Click **Choose File** or drag and drop image
2. Preview the image
3. Click **Save** to apply

---

## Serialization

In this option you can modify the following serialization fields:

![Serialization Settings](assets/image04.png)

### Laser
Indicate when the model uses Laser marking.

### LabelByPanel
Enable label printing by panel instead of individual pieces.

### LabelsID
Enter the label template name to be used.

![Label Configuration](assets/image05.png)

### PiecesBy Panel
Number of pieces per panel.

### Merge Serials
Enable merging of serial numbers.

### Generate Panel Master Automatically
Generate the panel master serial when serializing individual pieces.

![Panel Settings](assets/image06.png)

### PartSerialFormatEasy
Individual serial number format (regex pattern).

### PartSerialPanelFormatEasy
Panel serial number format (regex pattern).

---

## Tools Validation

In this option you can add:

Add the piece price for cost tracking.

Add the piece cost.

Enable scanning in PVS (Packout Verification Software).

![PVS Settings](assets/image09.png)

### BoxSize
Number of pieces per box.

### Layers
Number of layers per box.

### Layers_Allow_Duplicate
Allow duplicate serial numbers in different layers.

![Box Configuration](assets/image10.png)

### PackRowSize
Number of pieces per row in packing.

### Fense Required Before Packout
Minimum time required after testing before packing (in hours).

### Default Stock Location
Default inventory location after packing.

### PackType
Type of scan to perform:
- **Serial** - Scan individual serials
- **Quantity** - Scan by quantity
- **Panel** - Scan panel serials

![Pack Type Selection](assets/image11.png)

### Quantity Per Scan
Number of pieces to add per scan when using Quantity pack type.

---

### Serial Modify Options

#### Substring Option

![Substring Configuration](assets/image12.png)

In this option, the serial number at all stations might be different from the serial at packing.

**Example:**
- Station serial: `C310820101381`
- Packing serial: `#80A906093C    ##812774456#060920*X57 A3B3003FVG3*=300820102045C80A`

With this option, BeFirst can read part of the serial:

**Configuration:**
- **Area**: Select `PackOUT`
- **Format**: Enter `(51,2)` which means:
  - Position 51 = Start position
  - 2 = Total characters to read

![Substring Example](assets/image13.png)

#### Supstring Option

![Supstring Configuration](assets/image14.png)

Following the previous example, if you need to add a prefix to the extracted serial:

**Configuration:**
- **Area**: Select `Packout`
- **SupString**: Enter `C{SN}` (adds "C" before the serial number)

This option is commonly used for AUDI parts.

### Partial Boxes Configuration

![Partial Boxes](assets/image15.png)

**Steps:**

Number of empty trays or lids at the beginning of the box.

Define whether tray or lid will be scanned at beginning.

Number of empty trays or lids at the end of the box.

# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration

**Document ID:** IT-030 W1
**Application:** BeFirst
**Owner:** IT Department
**Status:** Active
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11

---

## Document Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |
| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |
| **Version** | A |
| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |
| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |

---

## Table of Contents

1. [General](#general)
2. [Image](#image)
3. [Serialization](#serialization)
4. [Tools Validation](#tools-validation)
5. [Packout Options](#packout-options)
6. [Areas by Panel](#areas-by-panel)
7. [Areas with Gages](#areas-with-gages)
8. [Areas Formats](#areas-formats)
9. [Areas with Pallets](#areas-with-pallets)
10. [Areas Locks](#areas-locks)
11. [2 Min Defects](#2-min-defects)
12. [2 Custom Defects](#2-custom-defects)
13. [2 Row Label Test](#2-row-label-test)
14. [2 Reprint Options Validations](#2-reprint-options-validations)
15. [2 Label Shipping Validation](#2-label-shipping-validation)
16. [BOM](#bom)
17. [Print Option](#print-option)
18. [Packout Alternate](#packout-alternate)
19. [Approved Stations](#approved-stations)
20. [Parameter Stations](#parameter-stations)
21. [Routing](#routing)
22. [Rates](#rates)
23. [Assembly Instructions](#assembly-instructions)
24. [Groups](#groups)
25. [Bins](#bins)
26. [Group Bins](#group-bins)
27. [Printers](#printers)
28. [Force AOI](#force-aoi)
29. [Search AOI](#search-aoi)

---

## General

In this option you can modify the following fields:

### PartType
- **Component** = Component part number
- **Wip** = Shop floor part number

![General Settings](assets/image01.png)

### CustomerID
Change the customer assigned to the part number.

### ISactive
Deactivate or activate the part number.

### Facility
Choose between Juarez (JUA1/JUA2) or El Paso.

![Facility Selection](assets/image02.png)

### PartDesc
Add a description to the part number.

---

## Image

This option allows you to add an image to the product. Simply select the image and click the **Save** button.

![Image Upload Section](assets/image03.png)

**Steps:**
1. Click **Choose File** or drag and drop image
2. Preview the image
3. Click **Save** to apply

---

## Serialization

In this option you can modify the following serialization fields:

![Serialization Settings](assets/image04.png)

### Laser
Indicate when the model uses Laser marking.

### LabelByPanel
Enable label printing by panel instead of individual pieces.

### LabelsID
Enter the label template name to be used.

![Label Configuration](assets/image05.png)

### PiecesBy Panel
Number of pieces per panel.

### Merge Serials
Enable merging of serial numbers.

### Generate Panel Master Automatically
Generate the panel master serial when serializing individual pieces.

![Panel Settings](assets/image06.png)

### PartSerialFormatEasy
Individual serial number format (regex pattern).

### PartSerialPanelFormatEasy
Panel serial number format (regex pattern).

---

## Tools Validation

In this option you can add:
- Solder paste validations
- Conformal coating requirements
- Parameter sheets

![Tools Validation](assets/image07.png)

Select the required tools and validation parameters for the part number.

---

## Packout Options

Configure packing and shipping settings:

![Packout Options Overview](assets/image08.png)

input.content:
"""
Properly extract DOCX to Markdown with images in correct locations
Organizes images in document-specific folder
"""
from docx import Document
from docx.oxml import parse_xml
import os
import re

def get_paragraph_image_rids(paragraph):
    """Get image relationship IDs from a paragraph"""
    rids = []
    for run in paragraph.runs:
        # Look for image references in the run
        drawings = run._element.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main}drawing')
        for drawing in drawings:
            blips = drawing.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}blip')
            for blip in blips:
                embed = blip.get('{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships}embed')
                if embed:
                    rids.append(embed)
    return rids

def extract_docx_with_image_positions(docx_path, image_output_dir):
    """
    Extract DOCX content and track where images actually appear
    Returns: list of (paragraph_text, paragraph_style, image_files)
    """
    doc = Document(docx_path)
    os.makedirs(image_output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # First, extract all images and map rId to filename
    rid_to_filename = {}
    image_counter = 1

    for rel_id, rel in doc.part.rels.items():
        if "image" in rel.target_ref:
            image_part = rel.target_part
            image_bytes = image_part.blob
            ext = rel.target_ref.split('.')[-1]
            filename = f"image{image_counter:02d}.{ext}"

            filepath = os.path.join(image_output_dir, filename)
            with open(filepath, 'wb') as f:
                f.write(image_bytes)

            rid_to_filename[rel_id] = filename
            print(f"Extracted: {filename} (rId: {rel_id})")
            image_counter += 1

    # Now go through paragraphs and track which images appear where
    content_structure = []

    for para in doc.paragraphs:
        text = para.text.strip()
        style = para.style.name if para.style else "Normal"

        # Get images in this paragraph
        image_rids = get_paragraph_image_rids(para)
        image_files = [rid_to_filename.get(rid) for rid in image_rids if rid in rid_to_filename]

        content_structure.append({
            'text': text,
            'style': style,
            'images': image_files
        })

    # Also process tables
    for table in doc.tables:
        for row in table.rows:
            for cell in row.cells:
                for para in cell.paragraphs:
                    text = para.text.strip()
                    if text:
                        content_structure.append({
                            'text': text,
                            'style': 'Table',
                            'images': []
                        })

    return content_structure, len(rid_to_filename)

# Translation mappings
translations = {
    # Headers
    "General": "General",

def translate(text):
    """Translate text with fallback"""
    return translations.get(text.strip(), text)

def create_markdown_from_structure(content_structure, image_folder_name):
    """Create markdown from content structure"""

    md_lines = []

    # Header
    md_lines.extend([
        "# IT-030 W1 - BeFirst Part Number Configuration",
        "",
        "**Document ID:** IT-030 W1  ",
        "**Application:** BeFirst  ",
        "**Owner:** IT Department  ",
        "**Status:** Active  ",
        "**Last Updated:** 2026-05-11",
        "",
        "---",
        "",
        "## Document Summary",
        "",
        "| Field | Value |",
        "|-------|-------|",
        "| **Document Name** | Part Number Configuration Manual |",
        "| **Objective** | Guide to configure part numbers in BeFirst system |",
        "| **Version** | A |",
        "| **Date** | 9/4/2023 |",
        "| **Author** | Jose Grajeda |",
        "",
        "---",
        ""
    ])

    # Process content
    skip_next = False
    for i, item in enumerate(content_structure):
        if skip_next:
            skip_next = False
            continue

        text = item['text']
        style = item['style']
        images = item['images']

        if not text and not images:
            continue

        # Skip document summary/history
        if text in ['Document summary', 'Document history']:
            continue

        # Handle headings
        if style.startswith('Heading'):
            level = int(style.replace('Heading ', ''))
            translated = translate(text)
            md_lines.append("")
            md_lines.append(f"{'#' * (level + 1)} {translated}")
            md_lines.append("")

        # Handle list items
        elif style == 'List Paragraph':
            translated = translate(text)
            md_lines.append(f"- {translated}")

        # Handle normal paragraphs
        elif text:
            translated = translate(text)
            md_lines.append(translated)
            md_lines.append("")

        # Add images right after the text
        if images:
            for img in images:
                md_lines.append(f"![Screenshot]({image_folder_name}/{img})")
                md_lines.append("")

    return '\n'.join(md_lines)

def main():
    docx_path = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals\IT-013 W1 F9 Part Number.docx"
    base_dir = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Enterprise Solution Architecture\Enterprise Systems\BeFirst\01_User_Manuals_MD"

    doc_id = "IT-030_W1"
    image_folder = os.path.join(base_dir, doc_id)
    md_file = os.path.join(base_dir, f"{doc_id}_BeFirst_Part_Number_Configuration.md")

    print("=" * 80)
    print("Extracting IT-030 W1 with Proper Image Placement")
    print("=" * 80)
    print()

    # Extract content with image positions
    print("Step 1: Extracting content and images...")
    content_structure, num_images = extract_docx_with_image_positions(docx_path, image_folder)
    print(f"Extracted {num_images} images to {doc_id}/")
    print()

    # Create markdown
    print("Step 2: Creating markdown with proper image placement...")
    markdown_content = create_markdown_from_structure(content_structure, doc_id)

    with open(md_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write(markdown_content)

    print(f"Created: {md_file}")
    print()

    print("=" * 80)
    print("Complete!")
    print(f"- Markdown: {doc_id}_BeFirst_Part_Number_Configuration.md")
    print(f"- Images: {num_images} files in {doc_id}/ folder")
    print(f"- Images placed where they appear in original document")
    print("=" * 80)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
Properly extract DOCX to Markdown with images in correct locations
Organizes images in document-specific folder
"""
from docx import Document
from docx.oxml import parse_xml
import os
import re

def get_paragraph_image_rids(paragraph):
    """Get image relationship IDs from a paragraph"""
    rids = []
    for run in paragraph.runs:
        # Look for image references in the run
        drawings = run._element.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main}drawing')
        for drawing in drawings:
            blips = drawing.findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}blip')
            for blip in blips:
                embed = blip.get('{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships}embed')
                if embed:
                    rids.append(embed)
    return rids

def extract_docx_with_image_positions(docx_path, image_output_dir):
    """
    Extract DOCX content and track where images actually appear
    Returns: list of (paragraph_text, paragraph_style, image_files)
    """
    doc = Document(docx_path)
    os.makedirs(image_output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # First, extract all images and map rId to filename
    rid_to_filename = {}
    image_counter = 1

    for rel_id, rel in doc.part.rels.items():
        if "image" in rel.target_ref:
            image_part = rel.target_part
            image_bytes = image_part.blob
            ext = rel.target_ref.split('.')[-1]
            filename = f"image{image_counter:02d}.{ext}"

            filepath = os.path.join(image_output_dir, filename)
            with open(filepath, 'wb') as f:
                f.write(image_bytes)

            rid_to_filename[rel_id] = filename
            print(f"Extracted: {filename} (rId: {rel_id})")
            image_counter += 1

    # Now go through paragraphs and track which images appear where
    content_structure = []

    for para in doc.paragraphs:
        text = para.text.strip()
        style = para.style.name if para.style else "Normal"

        # Get images in this paragraph
        image_rids = get_paragraph_image_rids(para)
        image_files = [rid_to_filename.get(rid) for rid in image_rids if rid in rid_to_filename]

        content_structure.append({
            'text': text,
            'style': style,
            'images': image_files
        })

    # Also process tables
    for table in doc.tables:
        for row in table.rows:
            for cell in row.cells:
                for para in cell.paragraphs:
                    text = para.text.strip()
                    if text:
                        content_structure.append({
                            'text': text,
                            'style': 'Table',
                            'images': []
                        })

    return content_structure, len(rid_to_filename)
